metadata.title: Weekly BioBlitz Challenge
metadata.description: Go outside, photograph living things, and add your sightings. Each week the top collector and the best photo win a prize.

banner.message: The Weekly BioBlitz Challenge is live. Photograph nature and win prizes.

hero.description: A friendly weekly race to record nature. Photograph any living thing you find, add it to GainForest, and climb the board.
hero.program: Part of the Klarna AI for Climate Resilience program, with GainForest.

rsvp.note: Register so we can track your entries and send your prize if you win.
rsvp.registeredNote: We'll track your entries and send your prize if you win.
rsvp.error: Could not register just now. Please try again.
rsvp.postText: I'm joining the Weekly BioBlitz Challenge — {label}! Heading outside to photograph nature for biodiversity. 🌿🔭 #BioBlitz

prizes.title: Prizes every round
prizes.badgeNote: Plus a permanent winner badge on your profile.

mostObservations.title: Most observations
mostObservations.blurb: For the collector with the most valid sightings in the round.

bestPicture.title: Best picture
bestPicture.blurb: For the most striking nature photo of the week, picked by the team.

outside.title: Head outside
outside.blurb: Forest, field, or your backyard — any living thing counts.

upload.title: Upload your photos
upload.blurb: Add them on your My Observations page, or with Tainá — also on GainForest.

review.title: Check the species name
review.blurb: Confirm or correct the suggested name — your knowledge matters.

proof.title: Your data does more than win prizes
proof.intro: Every observation you submit gets saved to your account, so you can use it later as proof for your own projects.
proof.uses: You can report back to grant givers with a clear record of your work. You can also show partners and stakeholders that what you do is making a difference.
proof.outro: No extra work needed — just keep observing. Over time, this builds real evidence you can use whenever you need it.

metadata.title: Grants
metadata.description: Apply for grant programs that fund data and decision sovereignty for nature-based communities.

hero.title: Grants
hero.lede: Open grant programs for communities building data and decision sovereignty. Apply with one of your projects.

rewilding.title: Rewilding the Web
rewilding.description: A grant program financing AudioMoth recorders and other field data collection tools, so communities can gather their own biodiversity data on the ground.
rewilding.support: Supported by the Klarna AI for Climate Resilience Grant.

server.aria: Interactive illustration of field data collection tools
server.caption: Field data collection

audiomoths.title: AudioMoth recorders
audiomoths.description: Acoustic recorders that capture biodiversity sound in the field.

tools.title: Data collection tools
tools.description: Field kits to gather observations and evidence on the ground.

rewilding.appliedNote: Your application for {project} is now posted to the feed.
rewilding.postText: Applying to the Rewilding the Web grant with my project "{project}" — for data and decision sovereignty. 🌱

applyModal.successBody: Thanks for applying with {project}. We'll be in touch soon about the next steps.

interoperable.description: Ma Earth's community-backed funding round uses quadratic funding: many smaller donations are matched and amplified more than a few large ones. Applications have closed and the round is now live — browse the projects taking part and support them.

metadata.title: Apply for Ma Earth Funding Round 3 — Certs
metadata.description: Learn how GainForest communities can use Certs to prepare for Ma Earth Funding Round 3 and the $500,000 matching pool.

hero.title: Prepare your Cert for Ma Earth Funding Round 3
hero.description: Ma Earth is running a community-backed funding round with a $500,000 matching pool for nature-based projects. If your organization already documents impact through Certs, use your public Cert as a reference when you apply on Ma Earth.

matchingPool.label: Matching pool
matchingPool.description: Ma Earth uses quadratic funding: broader supporter participation can matter more than one large contribution.

matchingPool.label: Matching pool
matchingPool.description: Community support is amplified through Ma Earth’s matching model.

fundingRange.label: Project funding range
fundingRange.description: For specific, feasible nature-based work with clear reporting.

why.title: Your impact record should travel with your application.
why.description: A Cert gathers the project story, organization context, location, photos, and evidence that funders need to trust the work. It helps bridge verified community work to Ma Earth’s funding process.

includeItems.evidence: Photos, evidence, and documented impact claims
includeItems.location: Location or site references when available
includeItems.publicLink: A public Cert link you can include in your application

items.nature: Nature-based restoration, conservation, agriculture, water, soil, biodiversity, or community projects.
items.plan: A specific plan with goals, timeframe, reporting capacity, and authentic success claims.
items.fundingNeed: A funding need between USD $2,000 and $15,000 for project-specific work.
items.stripe: A Stripe-supported country setup or a fiscal host partner that can receive funds.

roundCloses.event: Round closes and funding is paid

flow.title: Apply directly on Ma Earth, with your Cert ready.
flow.description: Certs cannot submit your application automatically. Open the Ma Earth fundraise page, apply there, and include your public Cert link wherever it helps explain your project and evidence.

hero.title: My Projects
hero.description: Create and manage your projects. Each project tells the story of your work and can be supported by funders.

metadata.title: Manage Projects — GainForest
metadata.description: Create and manage your projects on GainForest.

hero.title: Create a project
hero.subtitle: Turn field work, restoration evidence, and community stewardship into a publishable project — without leaving GainForest.

name.label: Project name
name.hint: use a name people will understand

summary.label: Short summary
summary.placeholder: Local stewards are restoring forest plots, tracking tree growth, and sharing updates from the field.

story.label: Story
story.hint: optional — the work, methods, and outcomes
story.placeholder: What happened? How was impact measured? Who benefits and what happens next?

scope.label: Focus areas
scope.customPlaceholder: Add another focus area, e.g. Indigenous governance

people.label: People & groups
people.suggestionsEmpty: No matches yet. You can keep typing a name.

sites.label: Places
sites.hint: optional — attach places from your account
sites.empty: No places yet. Draw one on the map to add it to your project.

photo.label: Project photo
photo.formats: JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, or HEIF.

success.descriptionNamed: “{title}” is ready to share and to receive support.

deleteModal.title: Delete project?
deleteModal.description: This will remove “{title}” from your account. This action cannot be undone.
deleteModal.hint: Deleting removes this project for everyone.
deleteModal.prompt: To confirm, type the project name exactly as shown:
deleteModal.inputAria: Type the project name to confirm deletion

basics.title: What's your project called?
basics.subtitle: Give it a clear name and a one-line summary people will understand.

focus.title: What kind of work is this?
focus.subtitle: Choose the focus areas that best describe your project.

timeline.title: When did the work happen?
timeline.subtitle: Add a start date, and an end date if the work has finished.

story.title: Tell your project's story
story.subtitle: Share the work, the methods, the evidence, and what changed.

network.title: Who and where?
network.subtitle: Credit the people behind the work and attach the places it happened.

photo.title: Add a project photo
photo.subtitle: A strong photo helps people recognise and trust your project.

review.title: Review your project
review.subtitle: Check everything looks right, then create your project.

emptyHero.description: Projects bring your impact work together — people, places, time periods, and supporting photos or notes — into one page supporters can follow and fund.

publish.title: Show your work on the explore pages
publish.description: Publishing makes this account and all of its projects visible to everyone browsing GainForest.
publish.publishedHint: This account and its projects are visible to everyone browsing GainForest.
publish.justPublishedHint: Done! It can take a few minutes to appear on the explore pages.
publish.memberBlocked: Only organization owners and admins can publish.
publish.error: Publishing didn’t go through. Please try again later.
publish.unavailable: Publishing isn’t available right now. Please try again later.

deleteModal.title: Delete Cert?
deleteModal.description: This will permanently remove “{title}” from your account. This action cannot be undone.
deleteModal.error: This Cert could not be deleted. Please try again.

metadata.title: Project Certs — GainForest
metadata.description: Mint and manage the impact certificates for a project.

hero.description: Impact certificates (Certs) you mint from this project. Each one bundles proof — photos, places, dates, and contributors — so funders can support the work.

errors.projectUnavailable: We could not prepare this project yet. Refresh the page and try again.

empty.noCertsDescription: An impact certificate (Cert) turns this project into a fundable story — proof of one contribution, with photos, dates, places, and the people involved. Mint your first one to apply for funding.

linkExisting.title: Link an existing Cert
